title: Negative sentiment spike in Disney Dreamlight Valley

evidence.content: I cannot recommend this game. The amount of bugs that have been left unaddressed for years is absolutely ridiculous. I just lost an entire hour of progress because the building mode failed to save AGAIN. This is a known issue that the developers seem to have zero interest in fixing. They are far mor

CAD$52 for the base game, CAD$117 for all the DLC, for a total of CAD$170~ (before taxes!) to play the full game, and that's before you even touch the premium currency and insanely predatory FOMO-style and incredibly overpriced microtransactions the game is riddled with.

...Y'know, in a game that w

This game was cute and fun, until they added microtransactions & premium currency to a paid game, and then added paid DLC that is only $10 less than the base game. There are three DLCs like this, by the way, so if you want to experience the full game you have to shell out $130+. Sure, you could easi

For a Disney game you'd think they'd be inclusive of everyone. But after years of playing this game, paying money on this game, I am realizing this game is not for me anymore. As a man, you will not get any new outfits geared towards you. The rewards in this game are 90% for women, dresses, purses,

I’ve expected a cozy, fun game. Instead, upon the first launch, I’m greeted with mobile game aesthetic (specifically reminds one of EA - Sims freeplay). Now this part is just taste, it’s different for everyone. 

So I bought this game, noticed the dlcs and figured it’s alright, every game has them.
